content.full_text:
September 8, 1989

Doris Christen, City Clerk
City of San Mateo
330 West Twentieth Avenue
San Mateo, California 94403

Re: Your Request For Advice
Our File No. A-89-462

Dear Ms. Christen:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided to you on behalf of your councilmembers concerning the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/

Your letter, dated August 3, 1989, correctly summarizes the advice I provided to you that a councilmember may designate an existing committee from a previous election to be used for a future election as long as the committee will no longer receive contributions or make campaign expenditures in connection with the previous election. Also, a campaign bank account which existed prior to January 1, 1989, may be designated for a future election if the funds in that account were received in compliance with the contribution limitations. Please note that Section 85201 and Regulation 18521 require that there be one bank account and one controlled committee for each office and each election. A copy of your letter is enclosed for your convenience.

Please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5662 if you have additional questions.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Alice Hughes
Political Reform Consultant

Enclosure

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

FPPC

Aug 4 11 06 AM '89

OFFICE OF THE CITY CLERK

330 WEST TWENTIETH AVENUE
SAN MATEO CALIFORNIA 94403

TELEPHONE (415) 377-3420


August 3, 1989


Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA   95804-0807

Attention:  Alice Hughes

Dear Ms. Hughes:

Thank you for the information which you provided me via telephone regarding FPPC requirements for council members running for reelection in November 1989.

From our conversation, my understanding is that a council member who has an unterminated committee from a previous election may use the same committee and committee name (as long as it includes the candidate's name) when running for reelection in November 1989.  He or she may keep the same bank account from the last election and use the funds therein for this November election as long as the new contribution limitations have not been exceeded.  Campaign materials left over from a previous election may be used for the upcoming election.  Forms 501 and 502 must be filed with the FPPC in accordance with instructions.

Would you please send written confirmation that my understanding of the above is correct, or if not, provide clarification which I may convey to our incumbent candidates.

Thank you for your assistance.

Sincerely,

[signature]
DORIS CHRISTEN
CITY CLERK

DC:2202B

24-4602

FPPC

Aug 4 11 C6 AM '89

OFFICE OF THE CITY CLERK                                330 WEST TWENTIETH AVENUE
                                                        SAN MATEO, CALIFORNIA 94403

                                                        TELEPHONE: (415) 377-3420


August 3, 1989




Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA    95804-0807

Attention:  Alice Hughes

Dear Ms. Hughes:

Thank you for the information which you provided me via telephone regarding
FPPC requirements for council members running for reelection in November 1989.

From our conversation, my understanding is that a council member who has an
unexpired committee from a previous election may use the same committee and
committee name (as long as it includes the candidate's name) when running for
reelection in November 1989. He or she may keep the same bank account from
the last election and use the funds therein for this November election as long
as the new contribution limitations have not been exceeded. Campaign
materials left over from a previous election may be used for the upcoming
election. Forms 501 and 502 must be filed with the FPPC in accordance with
instructions.

Would you please send written confirmation that my understanding of the above
is correct, or if not, provide clarification which I may convey to our
incumbent candidates.

Thank you for your assistance.

Sincerely,

[signature]
DORIS CHRISTEN
CITY CLERK

DC:2202B

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

August 10, 1989


Doris Christen
City Clerk
330 West Twentieth Avenue
San Mateo, CA  94403

Re:  Letter No. 89-462

Dear Ms. Christen:

     We received your letter requesting confirmation of advice
under the Political Reform Act on August 4, 1989.  Your letter has
been assigned to our Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
for response.  If you have any questions, you may contact that
division directly at (916) 322-5662.

     If the letter is appropriate for confirmation without further
analysis, we will attempt to expedite our response.  A confirming
response will be released after it has gone through our approval
process.  If the letter is not appropriate for this treatment, the
staff person assigned to prepare the response will contact you
shortly to advise you.  In such cases, the normal analysis, review
and approval process will be followed.

     You should be aware that your letter and our response are
public records which may be disclosed to any interested person
upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Sincerely,


Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

KED:plh:confadv1

A campaign bank account which existed prior to January 1, 1989, may be designated for a future election if the funds in that account were received in compliance with the contribution limitations. Section 85201 and Regulation 18521 require that there be one bank account and one controlled committee for each office and each election.

sections.question_synthetic: Can a city councilmember reuse an existing campaign committee and bank account from a previous election for a future election, and what are the requirements for doing so?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC confirms that a councilmember may reuse an existing committee and bank account from a previous election for a future election, provided the committee no longer receives contributions or makes expenditures for the prior election and the funds comply with contribution limitations. The Political Reform Act requires one bank account and one controlled committee for each office and election.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document contains multiple pages including the original incoming request letter (Aug 3, 1989), acknowledgment letter (Aug 10, 1989), and final FPPC response letter (Sept 8, 1989). Extracted from the final advice letter dated September 8, 1989, signed by Alice Hughes on behalf of General Counsel Kathryn E. Donovan.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: May a councilmember designate an existing committee from a previous election to be used for a future election? May a campaign bank account which existed prior to January 1, 1989, be designated for a future election if the funds were received in compliance with contribution limitations?

CONCLUSION: A councilmember may designate an existing committee from a previous election to be used for a future election as long as the committee will no longer receive contributions or make campaign expenditures in connection with the previous election. A campaign bank account which existed prior to January 1, 1989, may be designated for a future election if the funds in that account were received in compliance with the contribution limitations. Section 85201 and Regulation 18521 require that there be one bank account and one controlled committee for each office and each election.

embedding.summary: The FPPC provides written confirmation of telephone advice to the City of San Mateo regarding campaign committee and bank account reuse by councilmembers running for reelection, confirming that existing committees and accounts may be designated for future elections subject to specified conditions.
